must.title: Accordion header uses button role
must.description: The title of each accordion section must be contained in an element with role="button", typically a <button> inside a heading element

must.title: aria-expanded on each header button
must.description: Each header button must have aria-expanded="true" when its panel is open and aria-expanded="false" when closed

must.title: Header button references its panel
must.description: Each header button must have aria-controls pointing to the id of its associated panel

must.title: Enter and Space toggle panels
must.description: Pressing Enter or Space on a focused accordion header must expand or collapse its panel

should.title: Wrap buttons in heading elements
should.description: Accordion header buttons should be nested inside a heading element (h2–h6) matching the document outline

should.title: Arrow keys navigate between headers
should.description: Down Arrow should move focus to the next header, Up Arrow to the previous

should.title: Home/End jump to first/last header
should.description: Home key should focus the first accordion header, End key the last

avoid.title: Missing heading wrapper
avoid.description: Don't place accordion toggle buttons outside any heading — loss of document structure breaks screen reader navigation

avoid.title: Forcing one-at-a-time without notice
avoid.description: If the accordion collapses others when one opens, make this behaviour obvious to users — unexpected state changes are disorienting

react.label: React Accordion

html.label: HTML Accordion
html.code: Section 1 content.

tests.title: Keyboard Expand/Collapse
steps: Press Enter again — panel should collapse

tests.title: Screen Reader Heading Navigation
steps: Use heading navigation (H key in NVDA/JAWS)
Verify each accordion header is announced as a heading at the correct level
